prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for phenol were computed with chemkit’s fukui-reactivity skill using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier “standard”) in the gas phase.

Method provenance
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic), f⁻ (nucleophilic), dual descriptor, most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites (Mulliken partition)
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density-fitting), PySCF backend  (chemkit)
• Geometry provenance: single-point calculations on the user-supplied geometry file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz (no further optimisation)
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
• Charge = 0 (neutral); multiplicity = 1 (singlet). N ± 1 states: cation (+1, doublet), anion (–1, doublet)
• Convergence: all three SCFs (N, N–1, N+1) converged; integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true
• Warnings: none reported in result JSON; integrity checks note sums of f⁺/f⁻ equal 1.0000 (expected), status OK
• Live engine log written during execution (path echoed at run time by chemkit; not included in JSON excerpt)

Key numerical results (atomic indices are 0-based, labels 1-based)
Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C) with f⁺ = 2.1028319
Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O) with f⁻ = 0.16201477
Sum over atoms: Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000 (charge conservation satisfied)

The headline value returned below is the largest electrophilic Fukui index (f⁺) identified. The complete per-atom f⁺, f⁻, f⁰ and dual-descriptor arrays are available in the output JSON written by chemkit.

Provenance: computed (this work).
